# SafeURL Go SDK

Go client for the [SafeURL API](https://api.safeurl.ai), for URL safety screening. Designed for server-side use (e.g. [gno.land](https://gno.land) and other Go backends).

**Generated from OpenAPI.** The client is generated by [oapi-codegen](https://github.com/oapi-codegen/oapi-codegen). The spec is **fetched from the running API** (Scalar at http://localhost:8081/openapi/json), saved as `openapi.json`, then codegen runs. With the API running: `bun run codegen:go-sdk` (from repo root) or `sh scripts/fetch-openapi.sh` (from this directory).

## Installation

```bash
go get github.com/safeurl/safeurl-go
```

**Using from the SafeURL monorepo:** In your Go project's `go.mod`:

```go
replace github.com/safeurl/safeurl-go => /path/to/safeurl/sdks/go
```

## Quick start

```go
package main

import (
	"context"
	"fmt"
	"log"

	"github.com/safeurl/safeurl-go"
)

func main() {
	client, err := safeurl.NewClientWithAPIKey(safeurl.DefaultBaseURL, "sk_live_your_api_key")
	if err != nil {
		log.Fatal(err)
	}
	ctx := context.Background()

	// Create a single scan
	resp, err := client.PostV1ScansWithResponse(ctx, map[string]interface{}{
		"url": "https://example.com",
	})
	if err != nil {
		log.Fatal(err)
	}
	if resp.JSON201 == nil {
		// e.g. resp.JSON400, resp.JSON402 (insufficient credits)
		log.Fatal("unexpected response", resp.HTTPResponse.StatusCode)
	}
	// Note: JSON201 is *interface{} in current generated code
	fmt.Println("Scan created")

	// Get scan result (poll in production)
	// Replace with a real UUID from resp.JSON201 if possible
	scanResp, err := client.GetV1ScansByIdWithResponse(ctx, uuid.MustParse("00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000"))
	if err != nil {
		log.Fatal(err)
	}
	if scanResp.JSON200 != nil {
		fmt.Printf("Response: %v\n", *scanResp.JSON200)
	}
}
```

## Configuration

```go
client, err := safeurl.NewClientWithAPIKey(
	safeurl.DefaultBaseURL,
	"sk_live_...",
	safeurl.WithBaseURL("https://api.safeurl.ai"),
	safeurl.WithHTTPClient(&http.Client{Timeout: 30 * time.Second}),
)
```

## API coverage

| Method (with response)                | Endpoint                       | Description                               |
| ------------------------------------- | ------------------------------ | ----------------------------------------- |
| `PostV1ScansWithResponse`             | `POST /v1/scans`               | Create a single URL scan                  |
| `PostV1ScansBatchWithResponse`        | `POST /v1/scans/batch`         | Create up to 50 URL scans                 |
| `GetV1ScansWithResponse`              | `GET /v1/scans`                | List scan jobs (optional `batchId` param) |
| `GetV1ScansByIdWithResponse`          | `GET /v1/scans/{id}`           | Get scan by ID                            |
| `GetV1ScansByIdAnalyticsWithResponse` | `GET /v1/scans/{id}/analytics` | Scan analytics (LLM calls)                |
| `GetV1CreditsWithResponse`            | `GET /v1/credits`              | Credit balance                            |
| `PostV1CreditsPurchaseWithResponse`   | `POST /v1/credits/purchase`    | Purchase credits                          |
| `GetHealthWithResponse`               | `GET /health`                  | Health (no auth)                          |

Response types are `*XxxResp` (e.g. `*PostV1ScansResp`, `*GetV1ScansByIdResp`) with `JSON200`, `JSON201`, `JSON400`, `JSON404`, etc. and `HTTPResponse`. Check the status code or the relevant JSON field before use.

## Batch scanning

```go
resp, err := client.PostV1ScansBatchWithResponse(ctx, map[string]interface{}{
	"urls": []string{"https://a.com", "https://b.com"},
})
if err != nil { ... }
if resp.JSON201 == nil { ... }

// Note: JSON201 is *interface{}
```

## Error handling

Use the response wrapper: `resp.HTTPResponse.StatusCode`, and for error bodies use `resp.JSON4xx` (e.g. `resp.JSON400`, `resp.JSON404`), which are typed as `*interface{}` and contain the decoded error JSON payload.

## Constants

- `safeurl.BatchScanMaxURLs` — maximum URLs per batch (50).
- `safeurl.DefaultBaseURL` — default API base URL.

## Regenerating the client

**Preferred (spec from running API):** Ensure the API is running (e.g. `bun run dev`), then:

```bash
bun run codegen:go-sdk   # from repo root
# or
cd sdks/go && sh scripts/fetch-openapi.sh
```

This fetches the spec from http://localhost:8081/openapi/json and runs oapi-codegen. Override with `SPEC_URL` if your API is on another host/port.

**With openapi.json already present** (e.g. from a previous fetch):

```bash
cd sdks/go && go generate ./...
```
